6 min read
10 hours ago

--

And I’m never going back.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

I still remember the first time I opened an Angular component I wrote two years ago.
It had NgModule imports 15 lines long. @Input() and @Output() decorators everywhere. A BehaviorSubject for state that could have been a single variable. A subscription I forgot to unsubscribe from — a memory leak hiding in plain sight.
I thought: this is the Angular way.
It wasn’t. It was just the old way.
When I started building ZyraUI — an open-source Angular component library with 60+ components — I made a decision: I would write it entirely with the modern Angular APIs. No NgModules. No RxJS for state. No @Input()/@Output() decorators. No constructor(private service: MyService) injection.
What I ended up with surprised me. Not because the code was shorter (though it is). But because it was cleaner in a way that changed how I think about Angular.
The Old Way Felt Normal Until It Didn’t
Here’s what a basic button component used to look like in Angular:
// The old way
@Component({
selector: 'my-button',
templateUrl: './my-button.html',
})
export…
